audits.scope: Morpho Blue core protocol (singleton lending primitive, IRM, oracles)
audits.summary: Morpho Blue received a clean audit with no high-severity findings; one medium related to rounding direction edge cases was acknowledged and mitigated.

audits.scope: Morpho Blue full codebase including periphery contracts
audits.summary: Competitive Cantina audit found no critical or high issues; medium findings related to oracle price staleness handling and share inflation edge cases.

audits.scope: Morpho Blue smart contracts — core lending logic and math libraries
audits.summary: Trail of Bits found no high/critical issues; medium findings addressed rounding and precision in interest accrual math.

audits.scope: Morpho-Compound and Morpho-Aave v2 (legacy optimizer layer)
audits.summary: OpenZeppelin audited the original Morpho optimizer layer; one high finding (access control on reward distribution) was fixed before deployment.
Omniscia found no critical or high issues in the Morpho-Aave optimizer; medium findings were remediated prior to launch.

audits.scope: MetaMorpho vaults (ERC-4626 vault layer built on Morpho Blue)
audits.summary: MetaMorpho vault contracts audited with no high/critical findings; mediums addressed curator role privilege escalation edge cases.

exploit_history.description: No material exploits or hacks of Morpho Blue or MetaMorpho have been recorded on Rekt.news or DefiLlama hacks tracker as of mid-2025. Morpho does not appear in the Rekt leaderboard.

dependencies.notes: Morpho Blue is oracle-agnostic — market creators choose their own oracle. Chainlink is most commonly used in production markets. Oracle risk is market-specific; a bad oracle in one market cannot affect others due to isolated market design. Stale price or manipulation risk exists per-market.
Morpho Labs ships audited oracle adapter contracts (ChainlinkOracleV2) that wrap Chainlink feeds with staleness checks; these are open-source and audited.
Core deployment is on Ethereum L1; inherits Ethereum consensus security. Also deployed on Base (L2).
Morpho is deployed on Base; sequencer downtime or censorship could delay liquidations, creating bad debt risk. Chainlink L2 sequencer uptime feeds are recommended but market-creator-dependent.

dependencies.provider: N/A — no cross-chain bridge dependency in core protocol
dependencies.notes: Morpho Blue is deployed independently per chain; no native cross-chain bridge is part of the core protocol, eliminating bridge hack vectors at the protocol level.

rationale: Morpho Blue's immutable singleton architecture is a deliberate security-first design choice — once deployed, core contracts cannot be upgraded, eliminating governance-attack and proxy-upgrade vectors that have plagued other DeFi protocols. The protocol has accumulated 6+ audits from top-tier firms (Spearbit, Trail of Bits, OpenZeppelin, Cantina) with zero critical findings on the Blue core, and has no recorded exploits on Rekt.news or DefiLlama's hacks tracker as of mid-2025. The primary residual risks are oracle quality (market-creator-chosen, isolated per market) and the MetaMorpho vault layer which introduces curator/role complexity and is newer/less battle-tested than the core.
